data.title: Globant's Plunge: Earnings Lockout
TomaGold Intersects 17.4% Cu over 0.4 m in a New North-South Zone on Its Obalski Property
data.description:
MONTREAL, Quebec — TomaGold Corporation (TSXV: LOT) (OTCQB: TOGOF) ("TomaGold" or the "Corporation") is pleased to announce the discovery of a new North-South zone on its wholly-owned Obalski property, located approximately 2 km south of Chibougamau, Quebec. The Corporation intersected 17.4% copper (Cu) over 0.4 meters (m) in the new zone, which is open at depth and along strike. This discovery has the potential to significantly enhance the overall resource potential of the Obalski property.

The Obalski property, which covers 345 hectares, including a 33-hectare mineral concession, has seen extensive exploration and drilling activities over the years. The property is known for its gold and copper mineralization, with historic production from the A zone between 1964 to 1972 and the D zone in 1984. TomaGold has been actively exploring the property since acquiring it, with a focus on expanding the known mineralized zones and discovering new ones.

The new North-South zone was discovered during the Corporation's ongoing drilling program, which aims to better define the A-Po Zone and test new targets identified by geophysics. The drilling program consists of 1,500 meters (m) of drilling on sections 120+00 E and 250+00 E, with holes oriented at -60° to -70° dip angles. The Corporation is awaiting results for holes OBS-22-013 to OBS-22-015, which are being reanalyzed and will complete this first phase of drilling.

The following table presents the best results obtained for copper, gold, and silver for this first phase of drilling:

Hole From (m) To (m) Length (m)* Cu (%) Au (g/t) Ag (g/t)
OBS-22-013 122.85 122.35 0.50 17.40 0.21 0.68
OBS-22-014 264.0 264.5 0.50 0.81 0.81 0.20
OBS-22-015 387.3 391.5 4.20 0.25 0.29 0.43

* The width shown is the core length. True width is estimated at 65-70% of core length.

The Corporation is pleased with the results obtained so far, as the first three holes intersected the target zones, namely the A, C, D, and A-Po zones. The A-Po Zone is particularly well represented in all three holes with passages of massive sulphides composed of pyrrhotite, pyrite, and chalcopyrite, which are generally contained in quartz-carbonate-chlorite veins. These passages are approximately 30 to 60 cm discontinuous over intervals of up to nearly 15 m along the core, all within a very fine-grained gabbro.

The Corporation will continue its drilling program on Obalski, with the second part of the three-hole program starting this week on section 120 E. The targets are essentially the same as those on section 450 E at similar vertical depths. In addition, the Corporation plans to conduct some drilling on Monster Lake East once the work on Obalski is completed. A forest intervention permit was filed this week with the MERN for work to be carried out before mid-April. The Corporation also plans to conduct a low-level Mag survey on Monster Lake West. The survey will be flown with lines spaced at 50-metre intervals at an average altitude of 20 metres above ground level for a total of 235 km.

The Corporation invites investors to view the interview and the following articles on TomaGold:

Video interview:
Junior Gold Report:
SuperCharged Stocks:
Insert image of TomaGold's Obalski property
Insert chart showing TomaGold's stock price performance over the past year

In conclusion, TomaGold's discovery of a new North-South zone on its Obalski property, with high-grade copper intersections, has the potential to significantly enhance the overall resource potential of the property. The Corporation's ongoing drilling program and exploration activities demonstrate its commitment to unlocking the full potential of its assets. Investors are encouraged to stay tuned for further updates on TomaGold's progress.

data.title: Indian Drugmakers: Tariff-Proofing US Dominance Through Innovation and Adaptation
data.description:
Indian drugmakers have long been the backbone of the US pharmaceutical market, supplying nearly half of all generic medicines. However, the specter of US tariffs has cast a shadow over this dominance. The Indian Pharmaceutical Alliance (IPA), an industry body, has reassured investors that Indian drugmakers can retain their US dominance even with tariffs, thanks to a combination of innovation, adaptation, and strategic planning.



The IPA's secretary general, Sudarshan Jain, has emphasized that India's pharmaceutical industry is well-positioned to navigate the challenges posed by tariffs. "India and the US share a long-standing, collaborative partnership in healthcare," he said. "Continued dialogue will help address the issue, so continued availability of affordable medicines remains a shared priority for both nations."

Indian drugmakers have several strategies to maintain their competitive edge in the US market:

1. Innovation and Product Differentiation: Indian companies are investing heavily in research and development to create innovative products and therapies. By focusing on biosimilars, specialty generics, and other high-value segments, Indian drugmakers can differentiate themselves and maintain their market share.
2. Investment in US Manufacturing: Many Indian drugmakers, such as Cipla, Glenmark, and Piramal Pharma, have existing facilities in the US. By scaling up these operations or establishing new ones, Indian companies can reduce the impact of tariffs by producing drugs locally.
3. Passing Costs to Customers: While Indian drugmakers may choose to absorb some of the increased costs due to tariffs, they may also pass on a portion of these costs to US consumers through price hikes. This strategy can help maintain profit margins despite higher production costs.
4. Expanding Exports to Other Markets: Indian drugmakers are exploring opportunities to expand their exports to other markets, reducing their dependence on the US market. This strategy helps to diversify revenue streams and mitigate the impact of tariffs in the US market.

In conclusion, Indian drugmakers are well-equipped to retain their US dominance even with tariffs. Through a combination of innovation, adaptation, and strategic planning, Indian companies can maintain their competitive edge in the global pharmaceutical market. As the IPA has emphasized, continued dialogue and collaboration between India and the US will be crucial in ensuring the continued availability of affordable medicines for both nations.

data.title: TXNM Energy: Strong Earnings, Increased Investments, and Optimistic Growth Outlook
data.description:
TXNM Energy, a leading energy holding company, reported robust 2024 financial results and refreshed its growth outlook, reflecting the company's strategic focus on grid modernization and high-growth regions. The company's strong performance, coupled with increased investments and earnings growth targets, positions it well for future success.

TXNM Energy reported GAAP earnings of $2.67 per diluted share and ongoing earnings of $2.74 per diluted share, meeting guidance expectations. The company introduced 2025 ongoing earnings guidance of $2.74-$2.84 per diluted share and increased its long-term earnings growth target to 7-9%. This optimistic outlook is supported by a projected 12% increase in regulated rate base, indicating substantial revenue potential from regulated operations.



The company's strategic pivot toward grid modernization is evident in its expanded 5-year capital investment plan, which increased to $7.8 billion, representing a 26% increase over the previous plan. This expansion includes additional Texas transmission projects resulting from the ERCOT Permian Basin Reliability Study, providing a clear path for regulated returns in the high-growth Texas region. The regulated rate base is projected to grow 12% compared to 2025 levels, further supporting the company's growth trajectory.

TXNM Energy's strong performance across segments showcases the effectiveness of its strategic positioning. PNM benefited from higher realized gains and increased customer usage, while TNMP saw improved rate recovery through various mechanisms. However, both segments faced challenges from milder summer temperatures and increased expenses related to new capital investments.

Several key factors underscore TXNM Energy's strategic positioning and support its long-term growth prospects:

1. Texas Transmission Projects: The company's focus on Texas transmission projects, particularly those stemming from the ERCOT Permian Basin Reliability Study, provides a clear path for regulated returns in a high-growth region.
2. Regulatory Pragmatism: The phased implementation of PNM's rate increase demonstrates regulatory pragmatism, balancing customer impact with necessary infrastructure investments.
3. Constructive Regulatory Environment: TNMP's performance through TCOS and DCRF mechanisms showcases the effectiveness of Texas's regulatory framework in supporting utility investments.
4. Grid Reliability and Resilience: The 26% increase in the 5-year capital plan focuses on grid reliability and resilience, positioning TXNM advantageously in both its service territories with clear regulatory support for necessary infrastructure investments.

In conclusion, TXNM Energy's strong 2024 financial results and refreshed growth outlook reflect the company's strategic focus on grid modernization and high-growth regions. With increased investments and earnings growth targets, TXNM Energy is well-positioned for future success, driven by its strategic positioning and the support of a constructive regulatory environment.

data.title: Eli Lilly: A Buy After Strong Q4 Earnings?
data.description:
Eli Lilly and Company (LLY) reported robust financial results for the fourth quarter of 2024, with revenue and earnings per share (EPS) growth outpacing previous quarters and the overall market performance. The company's strong performance was driven by the success of its new products, particularly Mounjaro and Zepbound, as well as the growth of its established products like Jardiance and Verzenio. Eli Lilly's pipeline progress, including the approval of Zepbound for obstructive sleep apnea and Omvoh for Crohn's disease, has also contributed to its future growth prospects.



Revenue in Q4 2024 increased 45% to $13.53 billion, driven by a 48% increase in volume, partially offset by a 4% decrease due to lower realized prices. The volume increase was driven by growth from Mounjaro and Zepbound, while lower realized prices were primarily driven by Mounjaro, partially offset by Zepbound and Humalog. New Products revenue grew by $3.15 billion to $5.64 billion in Q4 2024, led by Zepbound and Mounjaro. Growth Products revenue increased 13% to $5.95 billion in Q4 2024, driven by growth in Verzenio and Jardiance, partially offset by lower Trulicity sales.



Eli Lilly's pipeline progress, such as the approval of Zepbound for moderate-to-severe obstructive sleep apnea in adults with obesity and Omvoh for moderately to severely active Crohn's disease, has significantly impacted the company's valuation and future growth prospects. These approvals demonstrate the company's commitment to innovation and expanding its product portfolio into new therapeutic areas. The positive Phase 3 results for other pipeline candidates, such as pirtobrutinib for CLL/SLL and muvalaplin for heart disease, further strengthen Eli Lilly's pipeline and contribute to its future growth prospects.



In conclusion, Eli Lilly's strong Q4 earnings and revenue growth, driven by the success of its new products and pipeline progress, make a compelling case for investors to consider the stock as a buy. The company's commitment to innovation, expansion into new therapeutic areas, and strong pipeline position it well for future growth. However, investors should continue to monitor the company's progress and assess the market's reaction to its upcoming clinical trial data and regulatory decisions.

data.title: Weave Communications Inc: A Growth Story in the Making
data.description:
In the dynamic world of healthcare software, Weave Communications Inc (WEAV) has emerged as a standout performer, reporting robust Q4 2024 results that underscore its strategic focus and growth prospects. The company's earnings call on February 20, 2025, revealed a compelling narrative of revenue growth, improved operating metrics, and strategic investments that position Weave for long-term success.



At the heart of Weave's success lies its strategic focus on medical vertical markets and mid-market expansion. By targeting specialty medical verticals, Weave has been able to tap into new markets and drive revenue growth at twice the rate of the overall company revenue in 2024. This expansion, coupled with its mid-market strategy, has enabled Weave to add 3,995 net new customer locations in 2024, totaling 34,997 customer locations by year-end.



Weave's improved gross margins are another testament to its operational efficiency and strategic initiatives. The company has consistently improved its gross margins for 12 consecutive quarters, reaching 72.6% in Q4 2024. This trend is driven by operational improvements, expansion into specialty medical verticals, and the integration of payments into office workflows.



Weave's investments in AI, partnerships, and payments have further enhanced its competitive position in the healthcare software market. By offering advanced AI features, integrating with popular practice management systems, and focusing on payments, Weave has created a comprehensive, value-added solution that appeals to healthcare providers. These investments are expected to deliver returns in the form of increased revenue, improved margins, and a stronger market position.

In conclusion, Weave Communications Inc's Q4 2024 earnings call revealed a compelling growth story, driven by strategic focus, operational efficiency, and strategic investments. As the company continues to expand into medical vertical markets and the mid-market segment, it is well-positioned to capitalize on new revenue opportunities and maintain its competitive edge in the dynamic healthcare software market.

data.title: Alibaba Shares Jump 15% in Hong Kong to 3-Year High as AI Momentum and Meme King's $1 Billion Bet
data.description:
Alibaba jumped 15% in the Hong Kong market on Friday, marking the highest level since October 2021. The e-commerce giant's core business recovery, AI-supported cloud services gaining steam, and GME Chairman Ryan Cohen's $1 billion bet drove the surge. The stock translates to another 5% rise from its close in the U.S. market on Thursday.

The Chinese tech just reported revenue of 280.15 billion yuan ($38.58 billion) for the three months ended December 31, an 8% year-over-year increase. Net income was 48.945 billion yuan ($6.72 billion), a 239% surge from a year ago. Cloud revenue growth reignited to double digits at 13%, with AI-related product revenue achieving triple-digit growth for the sixth consecutive quarter.

The company also committed to aggressively investing in AI, pursuing the goal of artificial general intelligence. Eddie Wu, CEO of Alibaba Group, said that investments in AI over the next three years would exceed those made over the last decade.

Alibaba became the target of a major regulatory crackdown by Beijing, which began in 2020 when the company's financial technology affiliate Ant Group was forced by regulators to cancel its initial public offering. The parent company also faced anti-monopoly allegations, leading to a free-fall in its stock price.

However, macroeconomic fatigue and the AI spotlight have facilitated a comeback by the Chinese government. Jack Ma, the founder of Alibaba, who has remained largely out of the spotlight since 2020, participated in a rare private meeting hosted by Chinese President Xi on Monday. During the session, Xi encouraged private businesses to display their abilities and boost their confidence in a new era for their operations.

In January, Alibaba open-sourced the new generation multimodal model Qwen2.5-VL and launched the flagship model Qwen2.5-Max based on the MoE architecture, achieving leading positions in multiple authoritative benchmark evaluations. The Tongyi Qianwen Qwen model family has become one of the largest AI model families globally. As of January 31, 2025, the number of derivative models developed based on the Qwen model family on Hugging Face has exceeded 90,000.

Meanwhile, Apple and Alibaba have collaborated to bring the latter's AI service to Chinese iPhones, further enhancing its AI model's advantages.

GameStop CEO and billionaire investor Ryan Cohen, who triggered a historical meme mania on Wall Street back in 2021, also aggressively increased his personal stake in Alibaba. He now owns roughly 7 million shares worth about $1 billion, The Wall Street Journal reported Thursday.

Citing people familiar with the matter, the Journal said the sizable stake in Alibaba is a bullish bet on China's economic growth in the long run.

Analysts also praised Alibaba's current movements. We expect the outlook for BABA's e-commerce business to remain strong in the first half of 2025, driven by continued trade-in subsidies, Nomura said in a note on Friday.

The next three-year period will likely be the single period in which Alibaba makes the most concentrated investment in AI and cloud infrastructure build-out, Barclays' analysts noted.

data.title: Tesla's Global Expansion: A Strategic Investment in Nissan?
data.description:
In a surprising turn of events, a high-level Japanese group, including a former prime minister, has drawn up plans for Elon Musk's Tesla to invest in Nissan following the collapse of its merger talks with Honda Motor, according to the Financial Times. The proposal, led by former Tesla board member Hiromichi Mizuno, is being supported by ex-premier Yoshihide Suga and his former aide Hiroto Izumi. The group is hopeful that Tesla will become a strategic investor, as they believe it is keen to acquire Nissan's plants in the US.



Tesla's potential investment in Nissan could offer several strategic benefits, including:

1. Access to Nissan's production facilities in the US: Tesla has expressed interest in acquiring Nissan's US plants, which could help it increase its production capacity and reduce costs. This would be particularly beneficial as Tesla aims to ramp up production of its affordable models, such as the Model 3 and Model Y, to cater to a broader market segment.
2. Expansion into new markets: Nissan has a strong presence in various markets, including Japan and other Asian countries. By investing in Nissan, Tesla could gain access to these markets and establish a foothold more quickly than if it were to enter them independently. This would align with Tesla's global expansion strategy, which aims to reach new customer segments across the world.
3. Diversification of product offerings: Acquiring Nissan would allow Tesla to diversify its product portfolio, offering a wider range of vehicles to cater to different customer preferences and price points. This could help Tesla attract a broader range of customers and increase its market share.
4. Synergies in technology and innovation: Tesla and Nissan could potentially share technology and innovation in areas such as battery technology, electric powertrains, and autonomous driving. This collaboration could lead to faster development and deployment of new technologies, giving Tesla a competitive edge in the market.
5. Strengthening the electric vehicle ecosystem: By investing in Nissan, Tesla could help accelerate the transition to electric vehicles by expanding the charging infrastructure and promoting the adoption of EVs. This would be beneficial for both companies and the broader EV market.



However, Tesla must also consider the potential challenges in integrating Nissan's operations and workforce. These could include cultural differences, technological differences, workforce integration, supply chain and logistics, and branding and marketing. To address these issues, Tesla could establish cross-functional teams, provide training and upskilling opportunities, and develop a clear marketing strategy that leverages the strengths of both brands.

In conclusion, Tesla's potential investment in Nissan could provide strategic benefits such as increased production capacity, access to new markets, diversification of product offerings, technological synergies, and a stronger electric vehicle ecosystem. However, Tesla must also address potential challenges in integrating Nissan's operations and workforce to ensure a successful merger. As Tesla continues to expand its global footprint, strategic investments like this could play a crucial role in its long-term success.

data.title: CompoSecure's Spinoff Surprise: Stocks Dip 1.18% Premarket
"Primus Raises $6.5M for Privacy-Focused Encryption in Blockchain"
data.description:
Primus, a privacy-focused encryption layer project, has successfully raised $6.5 million in pre-seed and seed funding rounds, with lead investments from VanEck and others. The funding will support the development of the project's core technologies, including zkTLS and zkFHE, which aim to enhance privacy and security in decentralized ecosystems.

Primus is addressing the critical challenges of trust and privacy in the blockchain and decentralized application (DApp) landscape. By acting as the missing encrypted middleware between current blockchains and applications, Primus leverages advanced cryptographic techniques to enable secure and reliable interactions. The project's focus on verifiable and privacy-preserving data is essential for the success of future systems, such as AI agents and DApps.

The seed and seed+ financing round was led by Dispersion Capital, Symbolic Capital, VanEck, with participation from Samsung Next, Alchemy, Maelstrom, and others. The investment reflects the growing interest in privacy-focused technologies and the potential of Primus to revolutionize the way data is handled in decentralized ecosystems.

The successful funding round comes as the blockchain and cryptocurrency industries continue to evolve, with increasing emphasis on privacy, security, and interoperability. Primus' innovative approach to encryption and data privacy positions it well to play a significant role in shaping the future of decentralized systems.

data.title: Trump's CMS Pick: Billionaire Doctor's Healthcare Investments Raise Conflict Concerns
data.description:
President Donald Trump's nominee to lead the Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services (CMS), Dr. Mehmet Oz, has significant financial interests in for-profit healthcare companies that could potentially be overseen by the agency if he is confirmed. According to financial disclosure forms, Dr. Oz holds millions of dollars in investments in these companies.

Dr. Oz, a celebrity heart surgeon and former host of the daytime television show "The Dr. Oz Show," was nominated by Trump in December 2020 to lead CMS. The agency is responsible for administering Medicare, Medicaid, and the Children's Health Insurance Program (CHIP).

According to financial disclosure forms, Dr. Oz has investments in several for-profit healthcare companies, including pharmaceutical companies, healthcare services providers, and medical device manufacturers. These investments could potentially pose conflicts of interest if Dr. Oz is confirmed to lead CMS, as the agency is responsible for regulating and overseeing these industries.

Dr. Oz's financial interests in for-profit healthcare companies have raised concerns among some lawmakers and healthcare advocates. They argue that these investments could create conflicts of interest and potentially influence Dr. Oz's decisions as CMS administrator. Some have called for Dr. Oz to divest from these companies if he is confirmed to the position.

Dr. Oz has defended his financial interests, stating that he has no intention of using his position at CMS to benefit his personal investments. He has also pledged to recuse himself from any decisions that could directly impact his financial interests if he is confirmed to the position.

However, some experts have raised concerns about the potential for indirect conflicts of interest, even if Dr. Oz were to recuse himself from specific decisions. They argue that his financial interests in the industry could still influence his broader policy decisions and priorities as CMS administrator.

Dr. Oz's nomination to lead CMS has been controversial, with some lawmakers and healthcare advocates raising concerns about his lack of experience in healthcare policy and administration. His financial interests in for-profit healthcare companies have only added to the controversy surrounding his nomination.

As the Senate considers Dr. Oz's nomination, it will be important for lawmakers to carefully scrutinize his financial interests and potential conflicts of interest. They should also consider the broader implications of his nomination for the future of Medicare, Medicaid, and the healthcare industry as a whole.

data.title: Tax Season Turmoil: IRS Layoffs Threaten Revenue and Service
data.description:
As the tax filing deadline looms, the Internal Revenue Service (IRS) finds itself in the midst of a storm, with the Trump administration's mass layoffs threatening to disrupt revenue collection and customer service. The layoffs, targeting roughly 7,000 probationary workers, are part of a broader effort to shrink the federal workforce and restructure government priorities under the guidance of Elon Musk's Department of Government Efficiency.

The IRS, which had been beefing up its workforce in recent years to improve customer service and beef up collection of unpaid taxes, is now facing a significant setback. The layoffs, which began this week, are expected to make it harder for taxpayers to get questions answered and for the government to collect all of the money it's owed. The move has drawn protests from government watchdogs, who worry it will compromise the government's ability to go after wealthy tax cheats.



The layoffs disproportionately affect enforcement efforts, with many of the newly hired workers focused on compliance, customer service, and tax return processing. This includes auditing high-income tax cheats, executives who use business aircraft for personal use, and wealthy people seeking favorable tax treatment through Puerto Rico without meeting certain tax requirements. The loss of these workers could lead to a decrease in revenue collection, as wealthy individuals and large businesses may be more likely to cheat on their taxes without the threat of audits.

The layoffs also come at a time when the IRS was beginning to recover from years of underfunding and resource scarcity. The Inflation Reduction Act of 2022 provided the IRS with $80 billion over a decade to modernize technology and add staff, leading to improvements in customer service and enforcement. The layoffs threaten to undo these gains and set back the IRS's progress in addressing its long-standing challenges.



In the short term, the layoffs could lead to slower refunds and longer wait times for taxpayers seeking assistance. Vanessa Williamson, a senior fellow at the Urban-Brookings Tax Policy Center, warns that "we can expect Americans to experience a return to slower refunds, to longer waits on hold, to dropped calls. It's going to be a real impact on customer service right as taxes are due this year." This could lead to increased taxpayer frustration and stress, as well as potential legal challenges.

In the long term, the layoffs could exacerbate the IRS's existing problems with high attrition rates and an aging workforce. Nearly two-thirds of IRS employees are eligible to retire in the next six years, and the layoffs could accelerate this trend, leading to further staffing shortages and a lack of institutional knowledge. This could result in a less efficient and effective tax collection agency, ultimately harming the U.S. tax system and the economy.

Moreover, the layoffs could have broader economic implications, as they disrupt local economies and hurt middle-income families in every state. The IRS workforce is predominantly outside the Washington D.C. area, and the layoffs could have a significant impact on these communities.

In conclusion, the layoffs at the IRS align with the Trump administration's broader goals of shrinking the federal workforce and restructuring government priorities. However, the potential long-term implications for the IRS and the U.S. tax system are significant, including a decrease in revenue collection, hurt customer service, and a less efficient and effective tax collection agency. The layoffs could also have broader economic implications, disrupting local economies and hurting middle-income families. As the tax filing deadline approaches, taxpayers and the broader economy may face significant challenges as a result of these layoffs.

data.title: Williams vs. Kinder Morgan: A Dividend Showdown
data.description:
Alright, fellow income investors, let's dive into the world of dividends and compare two heavy hitters in the midstream energy sector: Williams Companies (WMB) and Kinder Morgan (KMI). Both companies have been on a dividend growth spree, but which one is the better choice for your portfolio? Let's find out!



First things first, let's take a look at the dividend payout ratios and growth rates for both companies.

As you can see, both companies have similar dividend payout ratios and growth rates. However, Kinder Morgan offers a higher dividend yield, which is attractive for income-focused investors. Additionally, Kinder Morgan's expected dividend growth in 2019 and 2020 will result in a yield of around 7.5%, making it an even more appealing choice for long-term investors seeking income.

Now, let's talk about the growth prospects and expansion plans of these two companies. Both Williams and Kinder Morgan have attractive growth prospects and expansion plans, but Williams' dividend growth rate and long-term earnings growth target may give it an edge in terms of overall investment appeal for income-focused investors.



Williams has a large backlog of expansion projects currently under construction, which should add to its bottom line through the end of the decade. The company expects to spend about $1.8 billion on growth capital projects this year, with a long-term earnings growth target of 5% to 7% per year. Williams' dividend growth has been more robust in recent years, with a 5% compound annual rate over the past five years, including a 5.3% increase for 2025.

On the other hand, Kinder Morgan has a large set of expansion opportunities, with over $5 billion added to its long-term capital project backlog in recent months, increasing it by 60% over the past quarter to more than $8 billion. The company expects to spend about $2.3 billion on growth capital projects this year, with expansion projects having in-service dates through 2029. Kinder Morgan's earnings growth rate has reaccelerated over the past year, and it should really start hitting its stride in 2027 when the first of three major natural gas pipeline projects enters commercial service.

So, which company comes out on top in this dividend showdown? Both Williams and Kinder Morgan have attractive growth prospects and expansion plans, but Kinder Morgan's higher dividend yield and expected dividend growth make it a more appealing choice for long-term investors seeking income. Additionally, Kinder Morgan's reaccelerating earnings growth rate and higher projected dividend yield could make it more appealing for those seeking a higher income stream in the future.

In conclusion, if you're looking for a reliable and attractive dividend stock in the midstream energy sector, Kinder Morgan is the better choice. Its higher dividend yield, expected dividend growth, and reaccelerating earnings growth rate make it an excellent option for income-focused investors. So, go ahead and add Kinder Morgan to your portfolio, and watch your dividends grow and flow!

data.title: Alibaba's Q3 2025: Navigating Contradictions in AI Investments, Merchant Health, and Monetization Strategies
data.description:
These are the key contradictions discussed in Alibaba's latest 2025 Q3 earnings call, specifically including: AI Infrastructure Investment and Impact on Financials, Merchants' Financial Health, AI CapEx Investment Strategy and Profitability Impact, and AI Monetization Strategies:



AI and Cloud Growth:
- Alibaba's AI-related product revenue maintained triple-digit year-over-year growth for the sixth consecutive quarter, with AI Intelligence Group revenue growing 13%.
- Growth was driven by strong customer demand for AI products and the rapid adoption of AI technology across industries.

E-commerce Success:
- Revenue from Taobao and Tmall Group increased by 5% to RMB 136.1 billion, with customer management revenue up by 9%.
- This was attributed to increased investments in new user growth and enhancements in user experience, as well as improvements in merchant-friendly measures.

International E-commerce Performance:
- Revenue from AIDC grew by 32% to RMB 37.8 billion, driven by strong cross-border business performance.
- The international e-commerce business maintained strong growth, with improved operating efficiencies and investments in key markets.

Financial Discipline and Shareholder Returns:
- Alibaba achieved a 5% net reduction in share count over the last nine months through share buybacks, spending $1.3 billion in the December quarter.
- The company has been actively managing its balance sheet with strategic divestments and debt maturity extensions to enhance financial efficiency.

data.title: BAT Shares Plummet: FY2024 Results Spark Concern
Core Natural Resources Q4 2024: Unpacking Contradictions in Price Assumptions, Mining Resumption, and Tariff Impacts
data.description:
These are the key contradictions discussed in Core Natural Resources' latest 2024Q4 earnings call, specifically including: Metallurgical Coal Costs, API-2 Price Assumptions and Pricing Sensitivity, Domestic Contracting for Utilities, and Impact of Tariffs on China Sales:



Strong Start Post-Merger:
- Core Natural Resources reported significant progress in integrating its operations, with the capture of over $40 million in synergies in just a few weeks.
- The company also announced $1 billion in share repurchases to reward shareholders, emphasizing a strong capital return framework.
- The rapid integration and focus on synergies aim to enhance operational efficiency and create long-term value.

Leer South Mine Recovery:
- Core Natural Resources' Leer South mine successfully resumed development work with continuous miners over a month earlier than expected.
- This milestone was achieved through collaborative efforts with federal and state regulators, prioritizing employee safety and mine integrity.
- The swift recovery and resumption of mining activities are expected to contribute positively to the company's financial performance in the second half of 2025.

Coal Market Dynamics:
- Both API-2 and High-Vol A coal pricing is near three-year lows, reflecting a soft pricing environment.
- However, Core's strong committed and price position in the high CV thermal market and U.S. domestic thermal market tightening are offsetting these challenges.
- The U.S. domestic thermal market has seen a tightening due to cold temperatures, creating opportunities for spot market sales and strong contract market season.

Sustainability and Environment, Social, and Governance (ESG) Focus:
- Core Natural Resources emphasizes industry-leading sustainability practices, integrating this focus into its strategic vision.
- Post-merger, the company is well-positioned to capitalize on a constructive and durable market environment for its products, leveraging its skilled workforce, strategic asset base, and low-cost mining operations.

data.title: Autoliv Declares Quarterly Dividend: A Steady Income Stream for Investors
data.description:
Autoliv, the world's leading automotive safety systems supplier, has declared a quarterly dividend of $0.68 per share for the first quarter of 2024. This announcement comes as a reassuring sign for investors, as Autoliv continues to prioritize returning value to shareholders through consistent dividend payments. The company's commitment to dividends aligns with its strong financial performance and growth prospects in the automotive industry.



Autoliv's dividend yield stands at an attractive 2.83%, with an annual dividend of $2.80 per share. This yield is competitive with its peers in the automotive industry, such as Magna International Inc. (MGA) at 2.5%, Delphi Technologies (DLPH) at 3.2%, Aptiv PLC (APTV) at 2.1%, and ZF Friedrichshafen AG (ZF) at 2.7%. The company's stable dividend payout ratio of approximately 35% indicates that Autoliv is distributing a significant portion of its earnings to shareholders, providing a solid foundation for potential capital appreciation.



The declared dividend may have a positive impact on Autoliv's stock price, as investors who value dividends may be attracted to the company. However, the actual impact on the stock price will depend on various factors, such as market conditions, investor sentiment, and Autoliv's overall financial performance. Investors who prioritize dividend income may consider Autoliv as an attractive investment opportunity, given its stable dividend yield and payout ratio, which suggest a commitment to returning value to shareholders.



In conclusion, Autoliv's declaration of a quarterly dividend is a positive sign for investors, as it reflects the company's strong financial performance and commitment to returning value to shareholders. The company's dividend yield is competitive with its peers, and its stable payout ratio indicates a balanced approach to reinvesting in the business and distributing capital to shareholders. Investors seeking a steady income stream should consider Autoliv as an attractive option, given its strong financial performance and growth prospects in the automotive industry.

data.title: Prothena Plunges 8.2%: What's Next?
Altcoins Surge: Sonic, MANTRA, Story Hit New Highs
data.description:
The cryptocurrency market has been experiencing a bullish trend, with several altcoins reaching new all-time highs. Bitcoin's recent attempts to break out of consolidation have acted as a catalyst for this altcoin rally.

Three notable altcoins that have reached new all-time highs are Sonic (S), formerly known as Fantom (FTM), MANTRA (OM), and Story (IP).

Sonic (S) surged by 33% in the past 24 hours, reaching a new all-time high of $0.99 before slightly retracing to $0.94. The altcoin's recent performance reflects a bullish trend that could continue if key support levels hold and the momentum is maintained across the broader crypto market. Sonic is attempting to establish $0.81 as support, which is crucial for maintaining its uptrend. If this support holds, Sonic could push higher, aiming for the $1.00 resistance level in the near term. However, a pullback to $0.81 could trigger profit-taking among investors, causing the price to dip below this support and potentially leading to further declines.

MANTRA (OM) recently reached a new all-time high of $8.46, trading at $7.85 as of now. The cryptocurrency has been witnessing strong bullish momentum, suggesting that its upward trajectory could continue in the coming days. The altcoin is establishing $7.74 as a key support level, which is critical for maintaining its uptrend. If it successfully holds above this price, OM could see a rise toward $9.00. However, if OM price fails to hold the support of $7.74, it may fall to $7.20, revisiting the consolidation phase from earlier in the week and potentially extending losses for traders.

Story (IP) is another crypto token that has seen significant growth, with its price currently at $4.78 after a week-long rally. The altcoin achieved a new all-time high of $8.99 during the last 24 hours, representing a significant 174% increase. IP is now focused on securing $5.69 as a support level, which is crucial for its price continuation. If it successfully holds this support, IP could revisit the $8.99 high and possibly break above $9
